description: Ordered Bayer threshold matrices and error-diffusion kernels for palette-aware dithering. Bayer matrices are normalized 0..1 thresholds; for an input pixel value v in [0,1], pixel becomes black if v < threshold[y%n][x%n], else white. Error-diffusion kernels list (dx, dy, weight) terms to push quantization error into not-yet-visited neighbors (assumes left-to-right, top-to-bottom scan). Sum of weights = 1.

bayer_2x2.use: Cheapest ordered dither. Very visible cross-hatch pattern - works for retro feel.

bayer_4x4.use: Classic 4x4 Bayer. Visible 4-cycle pattern. Good for low-res pixel art with intentional grain.

bayer_8x8.use: Smoothest ordered dither for pixel art. Pattern still visible but less repetitive. Good for gradients in mid-res sprites.

bayer_16x16.use: Generated procedurally - matrix omitted to save space. Use only for HD pixel art (>=128px). At standard 16-32px sprites the pattern won't tile within the asset.

floyd_steinberg.use: The default photo dither. Spreads error to 4 neighbors. Slight diagonal pattern bias. Best general-purpose error diffusion.

jjn.use: Jarvis-Judice-Ninke. 12 neighbors, smoother than Floyd-Steinberg, slower. Good for high-res.

stucki.use: Stucki. Faster than JJN, similar quality. Less directional bias than Floyd-Steinberg.

burkes.use: Burkes. Single-row diffusion below current. Faster than Stucki, slight horizontal grain.

sierra_lite.use: Sierra Lite. Minimum 3-neighbor diffusion. Fastest error-diffusion. Slight diagonal bias.

atkinson.note: Sum of kernel weights is 6/8, NOT 1. The original Atkinson-on-Mac diffuses only 75% of the error - the rest is dropped. Result: high-contrast crispy look, blacks stay black, whites stay white.
atkinson.use: Atkinson (classic Mac). Loses 25% of error - high-contrast crisp result, prized for pixel art texture. Recommended starting point.
